hi.speaker: Keeper
hi.text: Oh — hello. Sorry. I didn't hear you. I was reading my own personnel file. I do that on breaks. It's the closest thing I have to a diary. Did you need the desk?

yourdesk.speaker: Keeper
yourdesk.text: Is it? Right. Of course. The roster says mine is by the window, but I keep sitting here. It feels rehearsed. Everything feels rehearsed since Day Zero. ...Did you start at this one too?

thinkso.speaker: Keeper
thinkso.text: 'I think so.' That's the answer everyone gives now, about everything. The records know. We comply with them. I stopped asking. It's easier.

why.speaker: Keeper
why.text: No reason. No reason. ...You hold your pen like you've held it for years. The rest of us had to relearn. That's all.

whoami.speaker: Keeper
whoami.text: A Keeper. Like you. Nine years, officially — it's in my file, with a photo of a woman who won a commendation in '31. I know she's me. I don't remember being her. You want the worst part? I know I have a daughter. I know her address.

variants.text: No. Not really. Every morning I read who I am before I can be it. And lately the file feels thinner. When that starts — find a quiet record and stay in it. It buys time. ...It isn't starting for you, is it? You always look like you remember.
I'm fine. Tired. It's a tired kind of job, being told who you are every morning. ...You'll see.

allofus.speaker: Keeper
allofus.text: Listen. You remember things. Don't say you don't — you walk in here like the building is familiar. If that's true, write everything down somewhere the Archive doesn't index. Paper. And if one morning I don't know you — reintroduce yourself. Please. I liked this conversation.
